'''David Barnhill''' (born 8 June 1969)<ref name=RLP/> is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who 
played as a [[Rugby league positions#Second-row forward|Second row forward]] in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. His club career was with the [[Canberra Raiders]], [[St. George Dragons]], [[Sydney Roosters|Sydney City]] and the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref name=RLP/>

==Background==
Barnhill was born in [[Wagga Wagga]], [[New South Wales]], Australia.

He was employed as a PE teacher at Kaleen high school in Canberra.

==Club career==
A Canberra junior, Barnhill made his first grade début with the Raiders late in the 1989 season as a replacement in a match against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. He became a more regular first grader the following year, primarily used off the interchange bench and was a member of the Raiders' victorious [[New South Wales Rugby League season 1990|1990 Grand Final]] side. By 1991 he was first grade regular and appeared in all 26 Canberra season and finals matches that year.

Barnhill joined the [[St. George Dragons]] in 1992 and was in the run-on side in 25 of the club's 26 appearances that season. He was a member of Saints' Grand Final sides which lost to [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]] in [[New South Wales Rugby League season 1992#Grand Final|1992]] and [[New South Wales Rugby League season 1993#Grand Final|1993]] and was still with the club for the [[Australian Rugby League season 1996#Grand Final|ARL Grand Final of 1996]] when he came on as an early replacement for Kevin Campion.

In 1997 Barnhill joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] and spent three years with the club where he mainly played in the [[Rugby league positions#Prop forward|Front row]]. The Roosters were serious Finals contenders in each of those years.

All up Barnhill took part in five first grade [[Grand final]]s (four of them in consecutive years) but with only one victory – his first appearance with Canberra in [[New South Wales Rugby League season 1990|1990]]. In ten of his twelve years of Australian first grade appearances Barnhill played in the Finals – exceptions being 1989 and 1994.

In 2000 Barnhill finished his playing career in a season with the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He cut short his two-year contract with Leeds at the end of 2000 and returned to Australia.

==Representative career==
Barnhill first represented for the [[New South Wales Rugby League team|New South Wales Blues]] in [[Rugby League State of Origin|State of Origin]] off the interchange bench in game I of 1994 and he appeared in all three games of that series. The following year he appeared in games II and III and he entered Origin folklore when footage of the all-in brawl of game II showed him standing and slugging it out blow for blow with Queensland's [[Billy Moore (rugby league, born 1971)|Billy Moore]] long after the rest of the melee had calmed down.
Following a two-year representative absence Barnhill returned to the Origin arena in 1998 and played in all three games. The last match of his nine match Origin career was game I of 1999.<ref>{{cite web|title=State of Origin player details|url=http://www.stateoforigin.com.au/soogame/PlayerDetails.aspx?Pid=85|access-date=30 August 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071026111805/http://www.stateoforigin.com.au/soogame/PlayerDetails.aspx?Pid=85|archive-date=26 October 2007|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Barnhill represented Ireland in the 2000 [[Rugby league#Rugby league worldwide|World Cup]].

==Post playing==
After retiring as a player Barnhill returned to Australia to take-up a position as defensive coach with the [[Brumbies (rugby)|ACT Brumbies]] rugby union club in 2001. In 2002 he was appointed as Assistant Coach to [[Nathan Brown (rugby league born 1973)|Nathan Brown]] at [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web| title=Dragons to move in new direction| url=http://www.showroom.com.au/dragons/sportrlnews/rlnews323.html| date=2 October 2002| access-date=30 August 2007| archive-date=31 August 2007| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070831105306/http://www.showroom.com.au/dragons/sportrlnews/rlnews323.html| url-status=dead}}</ref>

In 2005 he relocated to his home town of [[Wagga Wagga]] where he manages a hotel. He is the son of the former general manager of the [[New South Wales Country Rugby League|Country Rugby League]], David Barnhill, snr. In 2013 he coached the [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|New South Wales Country Residents]] on a tour of South Africa.<ref name=smh>[http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/government-to-recognise-south-african-rugby-leagues-status-20131020-2vuqz.html#ixzz2iQOhaXoz Government to recognise South African rugby league's status] ''smh.com.au'', 20 October 2013</ref>
